page: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Daldv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, which provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server includes 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The primary hardware tradeoff of this instance is its low memory-to-core ratio of 1:2, which prioritizes raw compute capacity and local storage volume over memory capacity. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D96alds_v6 offers high compute and local storage density relative to its memory footprint. This hardware profile is designed for workloads that require substantial parallel processing power and large local storage capacity but do not demand high memory-per-core allocations. Suitable workloads include batch processing, compute-bound tasks, local database hosting, and distributed file processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v6 is an x86_64 server instance from the Daldv6 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware configuration represents a tradeoff that prioritizes high compute density and substantial local storage over memory capacity. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-efficient option for parallel processing tasks that require significant local disk space without high memory demands. It is suited for compute-bound workloads, batch processing, and distributed data tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v6 is a Daldv6 family server with 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads requiring low memory-per-core ratios.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v6 features 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.
tagline: An AMD-powered server featuring ninety-six dedicated virtual cores, low memory-to-core density, and over five terabytes of local storage.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Designed for compute-bound tasks and batch processing
